steps.title: Identify the task goal
steps.content: The student must connect the Browner quotation to a SPECIFIC composition—not to a general observation or Ballard's overall style, but to an identifiable piece.

steps.title: Apply the quotation to the target composition
steps.content:
Choice A opens with the Browner quotation and immediately applies it to Cacéga Ayuwípi, using the Navajo water drum as an example of how that composition relies on Indigenous instruments.

The other three fail to pair the quotation with a specific composition.

This matches choice A—A is correct.
